senses.gloss: a half-breed from a male Bactrian camel and a female dromedary

examples.english: Their heads are inclined like the humps of Bactrian dromedaries.

etymology.text:
Back-formed and simplified from a nisba-epitheted name, as it has also been called الْبَخْتَرِيّ (al-baḵtariyy) الْإِبِل الْخُرَاسَانِيَّة (al-ʔibil al-ḵurāsāniyya), الْجَمَل الْبُخَارِيّ (al-jamal al-buḵāriyy) etc., various connections made to Bactria, Balkh and Bukhara.

From Persian بخت (baxt) or its earlier forms.

body.text: : (Msb, TA:) a foreign, or Persian, word, (Msb,) arabicized: (S, K:) or post-classical: accord. to the 'Ináyeh, not a chaste Arabic word: but in the Shifá el-Ghaleel said to have been used by the Arabs in ancient times; and the like is said in the L: Az says, “ I know not if it be Arabic or not. ” (TA.)
